title: Compute Cluster Scaling Headroom
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="vmware:cluster" earliest=-1h
| eval headroom_pct=round(100*(cpu_capacity_mhz-cpu_used_mhz)/cpu_capacity_mhz,1)
| where headroom_pct < 15
| table cluster_name, headroom_pct, cpu_used_mhz, cpu_capacity_mhz
description: Remaining vCPU and RAM in VMware/vSphere clusters and AWS ASG max — for **provisioning headroom** beyond host forecast (UC-20.2.1).
value: Remaining vCPU and RAM in VMware/vSphere clusters and AWS ASG max — for **provisioning headroom** beyond host forecast (UC-20.2.1).
implementation: Poll cluster aggregate capacity. Alert when headroom <15% or policy threshold. Trigger scale-out or new hardware.
visualization: Gauge (headroom %), Table (clusters at risk), Bar chart (by datacenter).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: vCenter TA, AWS API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:cluster`, `aws:compute:capacity`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll cluster aggregate capacity. Alert when headroom <15% or policy threshold. Trigger scale-out or new hardware.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="vmware:cluster" earliest=-1h
| eval headroom_pct=round(100*(cpu_capacity_mhz-cpu_used_mhz)/cpu_capacity_mhz,1)
| where headroom_pct < 15
| table cluster_name, headroom_pct, cpu_used_mhz, cpu_capacity_mhz
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compute Cluster Scaling Headroom** — Remaining vCPU and RAM in VMware/vSphere clusters and AWS ASG max — for **provisioning headroom** beyond host forecast (UC-20.2.1).

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:cluster`, `aws:compute:capacity`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): vCenter TA, AWS API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: virtualization; **sourcetype**: vmware:cluster. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=virtualization, sourcetype="vmware:cluster", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **headroom_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where headroom_pct < 15` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Compute Cluster Scaling Headroom**): table cluster_name, headroom_pct, cpu_used_mhz, cpu_capacity_mhz


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with vROps capacity dashboard; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (headroom %), Table (clusters at risk), Bar chart (by datacenter).
grandmaExplanation: Cost and capacity tools watch how much we spend on cloud and the data center, and where we have spare or strained capacity.
knownFalsePositives: Alerts during planned hardware refresh windows, before scheduled capacity adds.
